methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Stark Defence
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

private.name: Vatn Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $300M

private.name: Ares Industries
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

data.narrative:
Reveal Technology is a Series A–stage defense and security company founded by Garrett Smith that has raised $30M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of ten private companies and two public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) in the same sector. The three closest private comparables—Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M)—provide the primary same-stage anchors.

Two valuation methods were applied. The peer-median approach, based on the three Defense & Security Series A companies listed above, yields $300M, with a range of $150M to $500M. The stage-progression method uses a $1.0B proxy (derived from total capital raised) as a base and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implying $2.0B. The stage-progression method assumes inflected growth and successful technical validation between rounds; the peer-median method reflects actual recent market pricing for direct comparables.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $255M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $1.1B. This wide range reflects the core tension: peer comps cluster substantially below the stage-progression estimate, suggesting either that Reveal has achieved an exceptional fundraising position or that stage-progression assumptions require downward calibration. Key uncertainties include customer concentration, technical differentiation relative to the peer set, and the sustainability of defense-sector funding cycles.
